# Privacy Policy — Φάρος (Pharos)

**Last updated:** July 8, 2026

## About Us

Φάρος (Pharos) is a free, open-source, ad-free Android application developed by Gilberto Odino Pasquariello ("the Developer"). The complete source code is publicly available and verifiable on GitHub: [github.com/odino-pharos/pharos](https://github.com/odino-pharos/pharos).

This policy explains clearly and completely how Pharos handles data during use.

## The Core Principle

**Pharos does not collect, transmit, or share any personal data with the Developer or with third parties.** All data generated by the app remains stored exclusively on the user's device, in the app's local storage. No external server ever receives information from Pharos.

## Data Handled by the App and Where It Stays

### GPS Location
Pharos uses the device's GPS location (including in the background, when the app is not in the foreground) to detect movement speed and determine when to activate or deactivate mobile data blocking. **Location coordinates are processed exclusively in real time on the device and are never saved, logged, transmitted, or shared in any form.** There is no trip or location history accessible either to the Developer or to third parties.

### Driving Sessions
At the end of each driving session, the app locally saves (via the app's private storage on the device) some summary information: trip duration, kilometers traveled, start and end time. This data:
- remains exclusively on the user's device;
- is never synced, automatically exported, or sent to external servers;
- can be deleted at any time by the user by uninstalling the app or clearing the app's data from Android system settings.

### Local VPN Function
Pharos uses Android's VpnService API to selectively block data traffic from certain apps while driving (except navigation and music apps, which remain active). This function **operates entirely locally on the device**: it does not route, intercept, log, or transmit any network traffic to servers belonging to the Developer or third parties. Pharos is not a VPN in the traditional sense (it does not hide or reroute the connection elsewhere) — it simply uses the Android system API to selectively block data packets on the device itself.

## Permissions Requested by the App

| Permission | Purpose | Data sent to external servers |
|---|---|---|
| Location (fine/coarse/background) | Detect movement speed | None |
| Foreground service (location) | Keep GPS monitoring active while driving | None |
| Notifications | Show Pharos status while driving | None |
| Vibration | Signal events via haptic feedback | None |
| Auto-start on boot | Allow continuous operation after a phone restart | None |
| Battery optimization exemption | Prevent the system from interrupting Pharos while driving | None |
| Local VPN access | Selectively block mobile data as described above | None |
| Internet | Reserved for potential future features (e.g. update checks); currently unused for personal data transmission | None currently |

Pharos **does not request** access to contacts, camera, microphone, SMS, personal files, or Google accounts.

## Third-Party Services

Pharos **does not integrate any third-party SDK**: no analytics service, no crash reporting system, no advertising, no ad tracking, no usage statistics collection. The complete list of libraries used is publicly available in the `pubspec.yaml` file of the GitHub repository.

## User Accounts

Pharos **does not require any registration, login, or account** to function. The app is usable immediately after installation, without providing any identifying data.

## Donations

Pharos optionally offers the possibility to support the project via Ko-fi or PayPal. These transactions take place entirely on the external Ko-fi and PayPal platforms, according to their respective privacy policies; Pharos does not receive or process any payment data.

## Children

Pharos is not specifically directed at children and does not knowingly collect data from minors, consistent with the fact that the app does not collect data from any user.

## User Rights (GDPR)

Since Pharos does not collect or store any personal data on external servers, there is no Developer-held data on which to exercise rights of access, rectification, or erasure: **the user already has full control over their own data**, which resides solely on their device. Uninstalling the app or clearing its data from Android settings permanently removes all information generated by Pharos.

## Changes to This Policy

This policy may be updated in the future, for example in case of new features. Any changes will be published on this same page, with the last-updated date indicated at the top of the document.

## Contact

For any questions regarding this policy or data handling, the Developer can be contacted through the project's GitHub repository: [github.com/odino-pharos/pharos](https://github.com/odino-pharos/pharos).
